card.intuition: A method for testing integration points between services by ensuring they both adhere to a shared agreement (The Contract) without needing both services to be running.
card.analogy: A puzzle piece blueprint: service A and service B are being built in different factories. Instead of shipping the whole puzzle back and forth to check if they fit, they both use a scale drawing of the 'Link' (The Contract). If both parts match the drawing, they are guaranteed to fit.
when_to_use: Testing microservices developed by different teams
Replacing slow, flaky End-to-End (E2E) tests with faster integration tests
Preventing breaking API changes before they are deployed
gotchas: Consumer-Driven: Pact (the most popular tool) is 'Consumer-Driven', meaning the person USING the API writes the contract first. This can be a culture shock for backend teams.
Contract tests don't check business logic; they only check that the 'Shape' of the data matches.

exercise.prompt: Identify the popular framework used for Consumer-Driven Contract Testing.
exercise.test_code:
def check(ans): return ans.lower() == "pact"
assert check("Pact")
print("PASS")
